1: O LORD LORD , thou hast searched me, and known me me .
2: Thou knowest my downsitting and mine uprising, thou understandest my thought afar off.
3: And art acquainted with with all my ways.
4: But But , lo, O LORD LORD , thou knowest it altogether.
5: And laid thine hand upon me.
6: It is high, I cannot attain attain unto it.
7: Or whither shall I flee from thy presence?
8: If I make my bed in hell, behold, thou art there art there .
9: And And dwell in the uttermost parts of the sea;
10: And thy right hand shall hold me.
11: Even the night shall be light about me.
12: The darkness and the light are are both alike to thee to thee .
13: Thou hast covered me in my mother's womb.
14: Marvellous are are thy works; And that that my soul knoweth right well.
15: When I was made in secret, and and curiously wrought in the lowest parts of the earth.
16: When as yet there was as yet there was none of them.
17: How great is the sum of them!
18: When I awake, I am still with thee.
19: Depart from me therefore, ye bloody men.
20: And And thine enemies take thy name thy name in vain.
21: And am not I grieved with those that rise up against thee?
23: Try me, and know my thoughts:
24: And lead me in the way everlasting.
